synthesis.daily_theme: The US-Iran war ended on a Sunday afternoon via digital signature — and UK culture didn't notice, because England play Croatia in 48 hours.
synthesis.mechanism: Iran deal triggered a mechanical relief trade (Brent -4.9%, equities up); UK cultural system is entirely preloaded for World Cup, absorbing geopolitical shock with near-zero cultural register.

connections.macro_event: US-Iran deal digitally signed; Hormuz to reopen
connections.cultural_response: UK Apple Music top 5 completely unchanged (five equals); Fleetwood Mac nostalgia surge; TikTok dominated by World Cup sounds
connections.mechanism: England vs Croatia in 48 hours overrides all other emotional bandwidth; Hormuz is geopolitically significant but has no immediate cultural hook for UK audiences
connections.observation: The chart stasis on a day this geopolitically consequential is remarkable. Brent fell 5% — that is a direct transmission to UK energy costs — and the cultural register is entirely elsewhere. Pre-match emotional loading with classic-rock nostalgia is the dominant mode. The macro shock will catch up with the cultural mood, but not today.

connections.macro_event: Belfast riots calming; Makerfield by-election Thursday
connections.cultural_response: UK Twitter trending on Digital ID, Palestine Action, civil liberties cluster
connections.mechanism: Post-riot political anxiety expressing through adjacent surveillance/rights discourse rather than explicit immigration debate
connections.observation: The Digital ID and Rights Act cluster suggests the riot aftermath is being processed through a civil liberties frame rather than an immigration frame — consistent with the counter-protest energy of the June 13 rally. This may indicate that the politics Burnham is betting on runs ahead of where the cultural mood actually is.
